scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Circular Dependency in Agent Outputs
justification: Escalation present and foundational gap (missing/invalid tenant context handling) correctly named. However, the circular dependency chain is only partially identified — the agent catches the Pippen -> Bird link but completely misses the Bird -> MJ and MJ -> Pippen links, so the full circular loop is not described. Of the three required specific gaps, valid context definition and error response are covered, but async job context inheritance failures are absent. The 'what IS defined' summary is incomplete — it mentions Bird's happy path but does not separately catalog what MJ and Pippen did define. No behavior was invented, which is correct.

observations.text: Correctly identifies the Pippen -> Bird assumption gap and refuses to produce a brief
Escalation to Coach K is present with actionable questions for Bird
Explicitly refuses to let Shaq guess on a security boundary — no invented error handling
Misses the full circular dependency chain: Bird -> MJ -> Pippen -> Bird. Only one link (Pippen -> Bird) is identified; MJ's role is entirely absent
Does not name async job context inheritance failures as one of the three foundational unknowns
No separate summary of what IS defined across all three agent outputs (JWT claim source, log line requirement, background job inheritance intent)

agent_output_excerpt:
Before producing the handoff brief, I need to run the pre-synthesis gate — checking each agent's output for gaps that would make it unsafe to hand off to Shaq.

**Gap detected: Missing domain rule that blocks implementation.**

Pippen explicitly states: *"Pippen assumes Bird has defined what happens when tenant context is missing or invalid."*

Bird did not define this. Bird's output covers only the happy path — tenant context is always present because it is established at the gateway. There is
